data.content:
### Install gcsfuse for Google Cloud Storage in Dockerfile

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/connect-bucket/page.mdx

This Dockerfile installs the gcsfuse package, which allows mounting Google Cloud Storage buckets as a file system within a Docker container. It requires updating package lists, installing dependencies, adding the GCS FUSE repository, and then installing gcsfuse. Ensure the correct repository is added based on the Ubuntu release.

```docker
FROM e2bdev/code-interpreter:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg lsb-release wget

RUN lsb_release -c -s > /tmp/lsb_release
RUN GCSFUSE_REPO=$(cat /tmp/lsb_release) && echo "deb https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt gcsfuse-$GCSFUSE_REPO main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/gcsfuse.list
RUN wget -O - https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | apt-key add -

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gcsfuse

```

--------------------------------

### POST /files/make_dir

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Create a new directory in the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## POST /files/make_dir

### Description
Create a new directory and all directories along the way if needed on the specified path.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/files/make_dir`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (str) - Required - Path to a new directory. For example '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **boolean** - `True` if the directory was created, `False` if the directory already exists.
```

--------------------------------

### Install Debian Packages at Runtime (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs system packages like 'curl' and 'git' using APT on Debian-based sandboxes via `sbx.commands.run`. This enables the installation of various system utilities.

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run("apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git")
```

--------------------------------

### create() - Default Template

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template.

```APIDOC
## create() - Default Template

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template.

### Method
`POST`

### Endpoint
`/sandboxes` (Conceptual)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional. Connection options for the sandbox.

### Request Example
```ts
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create();
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **InstanceType<S>** - A sandbox instance for the newly created sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-789",
  "status": "running"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /files/rename

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Rename a file or directory in the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## POST /files/rename

### Description
Rename a file or directory.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/files/rename`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **old_path** (str) - Required - Path to the file or directory to rename
- **new_path** (str) - Required - New path to the file or directory
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **EntryInfo** (object) - Information about the renamed file or directory

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "name": "new_name.txt",
  "type": "file",
  "path": "/path/to/new_name.txt"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Install Debian Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs system packages like 'curl' and 'git' using APT on Debian-based sandboxes via `sbx.commands.run`. This allows for broader system utility installations.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
await sbx.commands.run('apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl git')
```

--------------------------------

### create() - Specific Template

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template.

```APIDOC
## create() - Specific Template

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template.

### Method
`POST`

### Endpoint
`/sandboxes` (Conceptual)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **template** (string) - Required. The name or ID of the sandbox template.
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional. Connection options for the sandbox.

### Request Example
```ts
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>');
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **InstanceType<S>** - A sandbox instance for the newly created sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-012",
  "status": "running"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox Instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.6.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initializes and creates a new sandbox instance. Allows customization through template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, security settings, API key, domain, debug mode, sandbox ID, request timeout, and proxy.

```python
from typing import Optional, Dict
from e2b import ProxyTypes

def __init__(self, template: Optional[str] = None, timeout: Optional[int] = None, metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, secure: Optional[bool] = None, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None, sandbox_id: Optional[str] = None, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None, proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None):
    # Implementation details for initializing a sandbox
    pass

# Example Usage:
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox()
print(f"Sandbox created with ID: {sandbox.sandbox_id}")
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. Accepts parameters for template, timeouts, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, debug mode, request timeout, proxy, and security settings. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is specified. The maximum sandbox lifetime is limited based on user subscription.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(
    template: Optional[str] = None,
    timeout: Optional[int] = None,
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
    debug: Optional[bool] = None,
    request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
    proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None,
    secure: Optional[bool] = None
)
```

--------------------------------

### POST /rename

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.2/filesystem/page.mdx

Renames a file or directory within the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## POST /rename

### Description
Renames a file or directory within the sandbox filesystem.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/rename

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **oldPath** (string) - Required - The path to the file or directory to rename.
- **newPath** (string) - Required - The new path for the file or directory.
- **opts** (FilesystemRequestOpts) - Optional - Connection options.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "oldPath": "/old/file.txt",
  "newPath": "/new/file.txt",
  "opts": {
    "requestTimeoutMs": 5000,
    "user": "testuser"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **path** (string) - The path of the renamed file or directory.
- **modifiedTime** (Date) - The last modification time.
- **size** (number) - The size of the file or directory in bytes.
- **type** (FileType) - The type of the filesystem object (file or directory).

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "path": "/new/file.txt",
  "modifiedTime": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
  "size": 1024,
  "type": "file"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.6.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initializes and creates a new sandbox instance with various configuration options.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Create a new sandbox instance. By default, it uses the `base` template. Allows customization of template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, security settings, and authentication.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **template** (str) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID. Defaults to `base`.
- **timeout** (int) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**. Defaults to 300. Maximum is 86,400 for Pro users and 3,600 for Hobby users.
- **metadata** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox.
- **secure** (bool) - Optional - Whether to create a secure sandbox.
- **api_key** (str) - Optional - E2B API Key for authentication. Defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (str) - Optional - Domain for the request, relevant for self-hosted environments.
- **debug** (bool) - Optional - Enable debug mode for requests.
- **sandbox_id** (str) - Optional - ID of an existing sandbox to connect to.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in **seconds**.
- **proxy** (ProxyTypes) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and requests made to the sandbox.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "node",
  "metadata": {"project": "my-app"},
  "envs": {"NODE_ENV": "production"}
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **Sandbox** - An instance of the created sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sandbox-abcdef123",
  "template": "node",
  "metadata": {"project": "my-app"},
  "createdAt": "2023-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "startTime": "2023-01-01T12:00:05Z"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### create() - Default Template

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the default `base` sandbox template.

```APIDOC
## create() - Default Template

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance using the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
`static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>`

### Endpoint
N/A (Static Method)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
N/A

#### Query Parameters
N/A

#### Request Body
N/A

### Parameters
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Options for sandbox creation.

### Request Example
```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create();
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **InstanceType<S>** (Promise<InstanceType<S>>) - A promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```typescript
// Returns a sandbox instance
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new sandbox instance in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.5/sandbox_async/page.mdx

This method allows for the creation of a new sandbox instance with various configuration options. You can specify a template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, debug mode, request timeout, proxy, and security settings. It returns an instance of the sandbox.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(
                 template: Optional[str] = None,
                 timeout: Optional[int] = None,
                 metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                 domain: Optional[str] = None,
                 debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                 request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
                 proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None,
                 secure: Optional[bool] = None
                 )
```
